contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped durable transaction and compliance retention controls
coreGuarantees: Saga initialization uses OCC versionPin:-1 before any step dispatch.
Saga step execution acquires SETNX step locks before step bodies run.
Compensation strategy registration happens before each forward step body.
Compensation executes in serial LIFO order and stops on first failure.
Compliance audit records are append-only and PLATFORM_ONLY.
Compliance audit hashes are SHA-256 fingerprints over tenant, saga, event, and write time.
Retention purge requires both expiry and absence of legal hold.
Retention purge archives before tombstoning the original record.
Every state event preserves store-before-enqueue ordering.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-19 governs durable transaction rollback and regulated retention behavior, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin:-1 before any step dispatch
SETNX step lock before step body
SETNX compensation lock before compensation body
ComplianceRecordWritten emitted only after storeDocument succeeds
retention expiry and legal hold dual gate

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-19 operating policy values. These values may vary by tenant without changing tenant isolation, saga idempotency, compensation order, compliance immutability, legal hold supremacy, or archive-before-tombstone safety.

keys.semantic: Maximum retry attempts permitted for one saga step before failure and compensation are triggered.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune operational tolerance while OCC initialization, step locks, and compensation registration stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Recovery time objective threshold for durable saga execution monitoring.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune operational alerting thresholds while step execution order and persistence guarantees remain MACHINE.

keys.semantic: Maximum allowed runtime for one compensation step before failure handling takes over.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune compensation tolerance while LIFO order, serial execution, lock acquisition, and stop-on-first-failure stay fixed.

keys.semantic: Retention window used to compute retentionExpiresAt when a compliance record is written.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune regulated retention periods while append-only storage, hash generation, and write-time expiry computation remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Cron schedule for retention enforcement scans.

boundedRange.allowedPattern: standard five-field cron expression or platform alias
boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune scan cadence while expiry, legal hold, and archive-before-tombstone ordering remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Maximum candidate records processed in one retention enforcement run.

boundedRange.rationale: Operators may tune retention throughput while the dual gate and archive-first guarantees remain fixed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to transaction reliability policy, retention policy, scan cadence, and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Regulated finance saga policy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A uses conservative retry and recovery thresholds for payment and ledger sagas.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T621 still initializes with OCC, locks every step, registers compensation before step bodies, and persists checkpoints before events.

edgeCases.name: Strict compensation window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A sets a short timeout for compensating failed transaction steps.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T622 still executes compensations serially in LIFO order and halts on the first failure.

edgeCases.name: Long retention compliance
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A extends compliance retention for regulated audit trails.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T623 still computes retentionExpiresAt at write time, stores append-only records, and preserves PLATFORM_ONLY scope.

edgeCases.name: High throughput retention scan
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A increases retention batch size and runs the scan overnight.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T624 still requires expiry plus no legal hold and archives before tombstoning records.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T621 through T624 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1554 through F1560 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Saga initialization must use storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin:-1 before any step dispatch.
Step locks must be acquired before step bodies execute.
Compensation registration must happen before each step body executes.
Compensation execution remains serial and LIFO.
Compensation failure stops remaining compensation steps.
Compliance auditHash remains SHA-256 over tenantId:sagaId:eventType:writtenAt.
Compliance retentionExpiresAt is computed when the record is written.
Retention purge requires both retention expiry and no active legal hold.
Retention archive must be written before the original record is tombstoned.
All state events preserve store-before-enqueue ordering.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

tenantARegulatedOperations.description: Tenant A tunes Durable Sagas and Compliance for regulated operational workflows.
tenantARegulatedOperations.impact: Tenant A gets more conservative retry, compensation, and retention policy while saga ordering, compensation ordering, compliance immutability, legal-hold safety, and tenant isolation stay unchanged.
tenantARegulatedOperations.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-19 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
